text.text: This hotel is an oasis in the midst of the businesses of Danang. Very peaceful and tranquil resort vibe whenever you want to unwind and chill, and in close proximity to restaurants, night market, and the beach if you want more action. Our room was fantastic - on the ground floor with a terrace and a swing overlooking the pool!  Bathroom was huge with a shower and a big soaking tub. The property was giving off Mediterranean vibes! Landscaping was beautiful! The breakfast was amazing! Banh mi station, Vietnamese coffee station and tons of other options. They have two pools so if you want more peace and quiet go to the rooftop! We also tried their hair spa package, which was a wonderful experience although I wish the spa opened earlier in the day. Staff was very helpful and friendly. Lisa at the front desk helped us with tours.. They also gave us a couple of great restaurant recommendations that have proven amazing!

text.text:
At first we had some minor issues . Luckily thanks to the great service and customer relations manager we were helped a lot whenever they could so that these issues were solved:

- The front door of the Junior suite we booked was stiff to open. After scanning the access card, you had to open the door by force. After addressing this they immediately fixed this.
- At check-in, we asked to shorten our 6-day stay to 5-day due to unforeseen circumstances. It took a few days for a definite answer but they were flexible enough to reimburse the last day.
- The suite turned out to have a 50L water boiler. Something to take into account when you try to have longer hot showers or try to fill the bath tub (you need to wait around 30min for hot water again). This was not something they could solve ofcourse but they were working on it to improve this. The suite itself was nice and spacious.

The hotel itself is very beautiful and the breakfast was great (lots of choice). Within 5min walking you are at the beach, so no need for a grab taxi. Should the weather be a bit cold for the beach then you can also swim in one of their 2 swimmingpools. All in all, a very good quality price ratio.

text.text:
Dear Management,
I am writing to you to share my immense satisfaction with my stay at the Risemount Premier Resort Da Nang.
The hotel is absolutely beautiful—extremely comfortable, spotless, and designed with such great taste. We truly enjoyed the fantastic breakfast every morning, the two stunning swimming pools, and the perfect location so close to the sea. It is simply a joy to be here.
However, I would like to specifically highlight the exceptional service provided by Ms. Lieu. In my eyes, she is the heart and soul of this hotel. Ms. Lieu is an incredibly dedicated, attentive, and caring professional. Her genuine hospitality and commitment to the guests' well-being made our stay unforgettable. It is rare to encounter someone who works with such passion and heart. She is a true asset to your team.
Congratulations on your wonderful resort and for having such an outstanding staff member.
Kind regards,
305 room Marki Gyorgy Márk

text.text:
We stayed at the Risemount for a total of 14 days to relax.

The location of the hotel is great – the city center can be reached quickly (about 10 minutes by taxi), and the beach is only around a 10-minute walk away.
The rooms are cleaned daily. We stayed in a suite, which offered plenty of space and really made us feel at home.

Now to the best part: the breakfast was outstanding. I have stayed in many hotels around the world and have never experienced anything comparable. The quality and variety are exceptional, and the whole experience is elevated by the first-class staff – incredibly friendly, helpful, and genuinely warm.

We had a personal contact person whom we could approach with any questions, concerns, or requests during the entire stay.
We were always greeted warmly and treated with so much care and attention. Truly wonderful.

Thank you, dear Risemount team! Keep up your heartfelt hospitality!

text.text:
I and my family visited the Nen restaurant to celebrate my mother’s birthday this evening. Unfortunately the low-standard food quality in this restaurant really watered down our spirits.
My sister had “the journey “ menu in August last year , she was so satisfied with the food. So we decided to fly all the way long from Hong Kong to celebrate my mother’s birthday. However they changed the menu to “Time”.
The starter we ordered it comes with durian broth which is so weird and not tasty at all.
The main course tasted equally bad. No matter what kind of the story you wanna tell, I think the food at least needs to be tasty.
We are so disappointed with the new menu. And the price is overpriced.we spent vnd 3,666,000 per person.

The only plus side was the well-mannered service that evening and the friendly way in which your waiters patiently heard our complaints.

An incredible dining experience in Nen. Our waiter Tho was exceptional, his knowledge and enthusiasm enhanced the evening. Chef Summer crafted a menu where each dish tells a unique story centered around the theme “Time”. The dish “Heal” especially stood out for me and perfectly encapsulated its meaning. Tho gave detailed explanation and backstory for each dish, making the meal not just about food, but a journey through storytelling.
The use of local ingredients was impressive. Highly recommend Nen for a creative twist on Vietnamese cuisine!

Every dish—from the sea urchin to the pheasant with glutinous corn—was thoughtfully presented and absolutely delicious.

Highly recommended!

text.text:
Went as a group of 7, spent 4M dong with drink set and sparkling water.

Nen’s lunch course set was amazing and a culinary experience. The server Tor was incredibly polite and took the time to explain each course in careful detail.

There is a drink pairing (alcoholic/non alcoholic) that is available at an add-on price.

I really LOVED the food and the experience. It’s definitely not cheap, but I felt that it was worth the charge for Vietnamese fine dining. The ingredients are unique to Vietnam and each dish is explained by the chef through story cards, which I enjoyed reading.

I would come again with loved ones who have not tried this, or if I am back in Da Nang with a new Story menu. Dessert was AMAZING.

Other than the price being a little steep, there is nothing not to love about Nen. Would recommend trying at least once!

text.text:
This is a Michelin Guide–awarded Vietnamese restaurant that specialises in bánh xèo (Vietnamese pancakes) filled with pork and shrimp.

They also serve bún thịt nướng (grilled pork noodles) and corn-filled fried spring rolls. I’ve been coming here for many years, even before they received the Michelin award, back when they were at their old location.

I’ve tried all of the dishes and they are all delicious. In my opinion, the secret is their house-made fish sauce — thick, rich, and clearly prepared in-house, not the thin bottled kind.

On my most recent visit, the pancakes were slightly undercooked and not as crispy as I prefer. I’d recommend asking for them to be cooked crispy when ordering to avoid disappointment.

Hải sản Mộc Quan, Danang, Vietnam. A casual seafood restaurant that is listed on the Michelin guide. Display of fresh seafood and water tank catches your eye as soon as you enter. text.text:
With more than ten thousand reviews and an average rating of 4.9, curiosity alone almost obliges a visit. And what can be said in retrospect is simple: the experience could hardly have been better. I arrived alone for a quick lunchtime snack and chose the king prawns with butter and garlic, along with summer rolls filled with barbecue pork.

Both dishes delivered immediate satisfaction. The king prawns carried a rich, aromatic balance between butter and garlic, allowing the natural sweetness of the shellfish to remain clearly present. The summer rolls offered a pleasant contrast through their freshness and delicate wrapper, enclosing a savory barbecue pork filling that was well seasoned and satisfying in its own right.

Service was swift and efficient, which suited the lively rhythm of a busy lunch service. Considering that the entire meal including a drink came to the equivalent of under thirteen euros, the value is frankly difficult to rival. Even at midday the place was notably full, a sign that its reputation is clearly well earned.

It is the kind of place that invites repeated visits simply to continue exploring the menu.

text.text:
​From the moment you step into Bếp Cuốn, you’re greeted by a beautifully designed setting that manages to feel both authentic and sophisticated. It is a total "well-oiled machine"—despite the extensive menu and the bustling atmosphere, the service is seamless, polished, and incredibly welcoming.
​The Culinary Highlights
​The food here is a vibrant celebration of Vietnamese flavors:
​The Showstopper: Our absolute favorite was the Fried Snakehead Fish with Mango. The contrast between the perfectly crisp fish and the bright, tangy mango was phenomenal.
​The Savory Classics: The Beef in Betel Leaf was truly awesome—smoky, tender, and deeply aromatic. We also loved the chicken wings, which were seasoned to perfection and incredibly tasty.
​The Perfect Refresher: We couldn't get enough of the Calamansi juice. We ordered a pitcher, and it was so refreshing and zesty that we immediately ordered a second!
​A Word of Advice
​While the decor and atmosphere make you want to linger, the secret is definitely out. We were lucky enough to be seated immediately, but by the time we finished our meal, a massive line had formed for lunch. Reservations are highly recommended if you want to skip the wait and dive straight into one of the best meals in Da Nang.

originalText.text:
​From the moment you step into Bếp Cuốn, you’re greeted by a beautifully designed setting that manages to feel both authentic and sophisticated. It is a total "well-oiled machine"—despite the extensive menu and the bustling atmosphere, the service is seamless, polished, and incredibly welcoming.
​The Culinary Highlights
​The food here is a vibrant celebration of Vietnamese flavors:
​The Showstopper: Our absolute favorite was the Fried Snakehead Fish with Mango. The contrast between the perfectly crisp fish and the bright, tangy mango was phenomenal.
​The Savory Classics: The Beef in Betel Leaf was truly awesome—smoky, tender, and deeply aromatic. We also loved the chicken wings, which were seasoned to perfection and incredibly tasty.
​The Perfect Refresher: We couldn't get enough of the Calamansi juice. We ordered a pitcher, and it was so refreshing and zesty that we immediately ordered a second!
​A Word of Advice
​While the decor and atmosphere make you want to linger, the secret is definitely out. We were lucky enough to be seated immediately, but by the time we finished our meal, a massive line had formed for lunch. Reservations are highly recommended if you want to skip the wait and dive straight into one of the best meals in Da Nang.

Recommend to visit! Must come for your Danang trip too!

text.text:
Here’s a clean, engaging, and positive rewrite of your review with a travel-friendly tone:

Ba Na Hills – Sun World is an amazing place and a must-visit attraction. We visited through a tour package booked at our hotel, which made the trip very convenient. Couples will especially enjoy this place — and as a bonus, you get one free beer at the entrance 🙃.

The atmosphere truly feels like a French colonial town, with plenty of fun activities to enjoy. There’s great food, beer, and entertainment, along with the long and scenic cable car ride, which is an experience on its own. The Golden Bridge is a dream spot for most visitors — perfect for unforgettable photos.

There are also many amusement rides, including exciting ones like Rock ’n’ Roll, making it fun for all age groups.

Tip: Visit as early as possible to fully explore the area and avoid crowds. Also, carry a light jacket, as it can get cool at the top.

Overall, it’s a fantastic experience with beautiful views, fun rides, and a unique European vibe — highly recommended!

text.text:
If I could give this place 100 stars I would. It was easily one of the coolest experiences of my 45 years of life. It is hard to put into words what this place is truly like , but it is magical and they are still building more!

First off it’s huge and takes a full day of walking and cable cars to see everything so keep that in mind when bringing children. It is also about 10 degrees cooler than down below so dress accordingly. They do accept Visa so if you don’t bring cash you will be fine. They also sell rain coats and sweaters just in case. The food there is definitely better than we expected and so was the service. You can get a Grab to bring you back from there so no need to hire a driver for the day, but if you decide to do that we paid out driver 650,000 which is about $25 usd and he waited for us and we paid him at the very end.

It truly is something everyone should experience once in their life.

It won’t let me post pics or videos on this for some reason, but you will mostly likely take a Ton of them. So photographers bring your set ups because it’s amazing!

Lastly GET THE WOW PASS. We got the silver and it was worth every penny.

There are various villages to explore, wonderful restaurants, and a host of adventure activities, including different types of swings and rides. The gardens are also beautiful, making it a memorable and enriching experience overall.

text.text:
A spiritual and cultural highlight in Da Nang – Highly recommended!
​After some rather "touristy" experiences in the city, the Marble Mountains were a breath of fresh air. This place offers everything I was looking for: deep culture, impressive history, and breathtaking views.
​The blend of natural limestone caves and beautifully integrated Buddhist pagodas creates a unique, spiritual atmosphere. Climbing the stairs is worth it for the panoramic views over the coast, but the highlight for me was the intricate stone carvings and the quiet energy inside the caves. It feels authentic and respectful of its heritage.
​If you appreciate history and want to experience the "real" Vietnam away from the flashy tourist traps, this is a must-see. It’s affordable, well-maintained, and truly memorable. 5 stars without hesitation!

Chùa Linh Ứng is a truly beautiful and calming place to visit in Da Nang. I went there as part of a tour, which made getting up the Son Tra Peninsula really easy and stress-free. The journey itself offers amazing views of the coastline and the city below.

Of course, the Lady Buddha statue is the main highlight — it’s massive and stunning in person — but the entire complex is worth exploring, from the gardens to the smaller shrines.

There are plenty of spots to stop, take photos, or just enjoy the quiet and the ocean breeze. Even with other visitors around, it still feels calm and well-organized.

I’d definitely recommend Chùa Linh Ứng to anyone visiting Da Nang, whether you’re interested in culture, spirituality, or just want a beautiful place with amazing views.

text.text:
This moment captures a visit to the Linh Ung Pagoda on the Son Tra Peninsula in Da Nang, Vietnam. The pagoda is a significant religious and tourist landmark, known for offering peaceful views of the ocean and city.
It is home to the tallest Lady Buddha statue in Southeast Asia, which stands around 67 meters high and overlooks the sea. The complex features a main temple hall, a bell tower, and a courtyard lined with bonsai trees and sculptures. Very beautiful! The site is open daily to visitors and entry is free of charge. If you want to enter the temple for pray, they require the visitors to dress respectfully, ensuring shoulders and knees are covered.

If you want to enter the temple for pray, they require the visitors to dress respectfully, ensuring shoulders and knees are covered.

text.text:
Oh my gosh—Linh Ung Pagoda (Lady Buddha) is absolutely beautiful. This was the perfect way to spend sunset—peaceful, grounding, and quietly stunning. The views are incredible, and it’s an easy taxi ride up, which makes it very accessible.

A few practical notes that are worth knowing: it can get cold and windy at the top, and if it rains, the marble stairs get very slippery—so go slow and wear good shoes. Also, shoes must be removed to enter the sanctuary area, so wear something easy to slip on and off.

There’s a lot to see here. If you actually take your time and explore, plan for 1–2 hours easily. Peaceful, beautiful, and well worth the visit. Don’t rush this one.

Bonus tip: the entrance to Monkey Mountain (Son Tra Peninsula) is right before the pagoda, so you could absolutely make a half-day of it if you want to keep going—add a few more hours and turn it into a full outing.

Organized, just very crowded. Not recommended to buy pasalubong here to bring home. You can haggle, but the marked down price is still more expensive than what you would find in Lotte Mart. They accept cash, credit card, and some foreign currency as payment.

Great place to get clothes and souvenir. Got two plus sized girlie dresses for 400k vnd, and two plus sized linen pants for 350k vnd after some haggling. Plus size girlie clothes typically range 1.5x to 2x more expensive than that where I'm from.

Ref magnets range from 100 to 150 vnd for a pack of six. Some more intricate designs go for 40 to 50k vnd. Again, haggle especially if you are buying a lot.

Good, affordable local fare sold on the right side of the ground floor, exiting to Han River. They may not speak English, but I can feel the hospitality and desire to show how the beauty of Vietnam. Our vendor saw how surprised we were by the size of their avocado and gave us a free taste.

ChIJLWoAs80ZQjERLKHDYD-ZymI.display_name: Da Nang Museum of Cham Sculpture
ChIJLWoAs80ZQjERLKHDYD-ZymI.formatted_address: 02 Đ. 2 Tháng 9, Phường, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
ChIJLWoAs80ZQjERLKHDYD-ZymI.editorial_summary: Compact museum displaying a collection of sculptures & cultural items like costumes & instruments.

text.text:
One of the most impressive museums in Da Nang and truly one of the best museums I have visited anywhere. The collection of Cham sculptures and temple elements feels so significant that the museum itself reminds me of a UNESCO heritage site.

The staff here are incredibly kind and passionate. When I told them how interested I was in Cham culture, they even found a book in Russian and helped me learn more about the history and mythology of the Cham civilization. That level of care made the visit very special.

The exhibition is beautifully organized. As you walk through the halls you can see how the space is structured almost like a spiritual journey — with guardian figures such as Yakshas and Dvarapalas watching over the halls with the deities. Deeper inside, you encounter what feel like real temple remains and architectural fragments, reminiscent of places like Angkor Wat.

The museum preserves such a powerful sense of history that you can spend a long time quietly observing each sculpture. I spent almost an entire day here and learned so much.

The only thing I would suggest is adding a little more information about the early formation of the Cham dynasty, especially in the first hall where the map is displayed. A short article or QR code with additional historical context could make the experience even richer for visitors.

Thank you for preserving this beautiful and important culture and sharing it with the world. This museum is absolutely a must-visit in Da Nang.

Tip for visitors: plan at least 2–3 hours for this museum. If you are interested in history, mythology, or archaeology, you could easily spend half a day here.

Highly recommend visiting in the morning when it is quieter — it gives you time to really appreciate the sculptures and the atmosphere of the museum.

text.text:
A pretty good museum to spend some time in.

The entrance fee is 60.000VND and you can spend as long as you like. There is a banner when you enter, where you can use the QR code to log into their WiFi.

There is another QR code below that, leading you to a website, where you can follow along (audio or text) and learn more about the larger, more prominent displays.

We spent about an hour perusing the 3 floors of Cham sculptures, mostly created from sandstone. There is also a room with photographs in chronological order, telling you about the history of the museum and its inception.

Fascinating history about the Cham civilization, its culture, and people.

When you're done with the tour, there's a little courtyard with a coffee shop. We got coffee (it was REALLY good coffee!) and sat underneath the banyan tree to enjoy the view of Dragon Bridge and Han River.

text.text: There are only a few cities around the world that I would want to revisit again and again, and Hoi An is one of them. Packed in a small area of ancient town are ancient temples, shops and restaurants, historical sites, unique structures, a river with lantern boats, night markets, bars and clubs, hotels and hostels and tens of thousands of tourists per day.  The people of Hoi An are extremely friendly.  You will find a good photo spot at almost every corner or alley ways.  This is what makes this town unique.  You can take picture until you run out of storage space and still there is more to discover.  At night is when all the people come out and all the action happen. There are lots to do and lots to see. Make sure to have seafood at the end corner of the night market, it is cheap and delicious.

text.text:
If there is one place in Vietnam that I would tell everyone not to miss, it is Hoi An.

During the day, the Old Town feels peaceful and timeless. The yellow colonial buildings, narrow lanes, and historic houses create a charming atmosphere that reflects its past as a bustling trading port. Walking around on foot is a pleasure — every corner feels photogenic and full of character.

But Hoi An becomes truly magical at night.

As the sun sets, thousands of colorful lanterns light up the streets and the Thu Bon River. The reflections on the water create a dreamy, romantic scene that is unforgettable. Taking a small boat ride and releasing a floating lantern on the river is a simple yet meaningful experience — calm, beautiful, and symbolic.

The people here are warm and welcoming, which makes the whole experience even more special. Shop owners and locals are friendly, patient, and happy to share their culture.

Another highlight is the tailoring. Hoi An is famous for its custom-made clothing. You can have a well-fitted suit or elegant dress tailored within just a few hours to a day, at very reasonable prices considering the craftsmanship and quality. It’s a rare combination of speed, skill, and affordability.

Hoi An is more than a destination — it’s an atmosphere. A place where history, culture, beauty, and hospitality come together effortlessly. A true must-visit when traveling to Vietnam.

text.text:
Hoi An is one of the most beautiful and atmospheric places in Vietnam. The ancient town, with its glowing lanterns, yellow walls, and riverside charm, feels like stepping into another world. Whether you visit during the day or at night, every corner has something picturesque and full of character.

The food is exceptional, the people are warm, and the mix of Vietnamese, Chinese, and Japanese heritage creates a unique cultural experience. Walking through the old streets, trying local dishes, taking a boat ride on the river — everything feels peaceful and unforgettable.

Yes, it can get busy during peak hours, but the beauty and atmosphere more than make up for it. If you’re traveling in Vietnam, Hoi An is an absolute must-visit — a place that stays with you long after you leave.

text.text:
Hoi An Ancient Town is well worth visiting if you can get past the heavy concentration of shops and focus on the history and atmosphere.

Take time to visit the old merchant houses and temples, which give real context to how the town developed.

In the early evening, the lanterns light up the streets and river, creating a beautiful setting. A short boat ride on the river and releasing a lantern adds to the experience.

There are plenty of cafés to stop for coffee, and the town is also popular for custom-made clothing if you have time. Ducking into narrow alleys often leads to quieter restaurants away from the main flow.

The area is very walkable and best enjoyed in the early evening, though you do need to stay alert for bikes and motorbikes.

Simply walking through the streets, sounds, and lights is an experience in itself.

text.text:
​If you think Hoi An is quiet, wait until you cross the bridge to the Nguyen Hoang Night Market. This place is a vibrant explosion of color and energy that is absolutely unmissable when the sun goes down. However, "peaceful" is the last word I’d use to describe it—the crowds are overwhelmingly intense, and you’ll find yourself shoulder-to-shoulder with hundreds of other tourists.🇻🇳
​The highlight, without a doubt, is the stunning display of silk lanterns. Hundreds of them glow in every shade imaginable, creating a magical backdrop that makes the chaotic queuing for photos feel somewhat worth it. It’s a photographer’s dream, provided you have the patience to navigate the sea of people.
​When it comes to the food, it’s a mixed bag. You’ll see endless stalls selling everything from banh mi to grilled pork skewers and the famous "banana pancakes." To be fair, the street food is nothing extraordinary—it’s mostly generic snacks meant for quick consumption. You eat here for the buzzing atmosphere rather than a gourmet experience.
​What makes it bearable is the location. Situated right by the Hoai River, the cool breeze and the sight of floating paper lanterns help soothe the exhaustion of walking through the packed aisles. It’s loud, it’s crowded, and it’s a bit commercial, but the sheer energy of the market is something you have to feel at least once. Just keep your bags close and your camera ready!🖤🧡❤

text.text:
Hội An Night Market is one of the most magical places in the entire city. In the evening, the streets light up with hundreds of colorful lanterns, creating a unique atmosphere unlike any other night market. It’s the perfect spot to feel the true spirit of Hội An and immerse yourself in local culture.
🛍️ Lots of stalls and great souvenirs
The market offers a wide variety of handmade items — beautiful lanterns, jewelry, crafts, and small gifts. Prices are reasonable, and bargaining is part of the fun.
🍜 Delicious street food
One of the biggest highlights is the food. You can try local specialties like bánh mì, tapioca snacks, fresh fruit, and famous noodle dishes. Everything is fresh, flavorful, and served with a smile.
🌟 Wonderful atmosphere
Walking through the lantern-lit streets, surrounded by music and the aromas of local cuisine, is a truly unforgettable experience. The market is lively yet friendly and feels safe — an ideal spot for a relaxed evening stroll.
⭐ Summary
Hội An Night Market is a must‑visit for anyone coming to Hội An. Beautiful lights, delicious food, colorful stalls, and a unique atmosphere make it a place you’ll want to return to. Highly recommended!

text.text:
The Lantern Night Market is absolutely mesmerising. The view at night is stunning, with lanterns lighting up the entire place and creating a magical atmosphere. The boat ride is a must-do—peaceful, beautiful, and perfectly timed for the evening glow.

The food options are fantastic, with something for everyone, and everything we tried was delicious.

Overall, it’s an amazing experience and the perfect place to spend your evening soaking in great views, good food, and vibrant energy. Highly recommended! ✨

text.text:
🎺If you wanna find somewhere to forget your boring life, this is exactly the right choice. You will see hundreds of visitors even walking together or sing songs with some amateur bands here. During lunar new year, the temperature here is perfect for a traveling since it is quite cool with breeze from the river. The temperature is even more better in the evening where you can see different colors of lighters from bars or restaurants.
✒️Note that if you find Asian foods or seafoods, this old town is not the right address. You should go to the Cua Dai inlet where is about 4-5km from here with relatively cheap prices and high quality.

Highly recommended.

text.text:
We recently visited this massage spa and was thoroughly impressed. The therapists effectively relieved our neck and leg pain, providing much-needed relaxation. The atmosphere was soothing, and the service was top-notch. As a bonus, they included a voucher for a dinner buffet, which was a delightful extra.
Minus one star because of the compulsory tip of 500k for foreign guests, which was not included in the price list

text.text: Came here out of the blue with no research. It was a recommendation from my grab driver. Front staff was very helpful in deciding the package best suited for me. It was very busy but managed to get a room quite quickly. The message was better than anything I've done back home. I would definitely go again next time I'm in Da Nang and would recommend any to visit.

text.text: This place is *amazing*. Recommended by my Vietnamese friend who lives nearby. I went for the deluxe treatment: 4-hands with soak tub and steam room. Didn’t know it but also came with a full facial - oils, face massage, brushed-on mask, etc. Entirely soothing. First, after the soak in the herbal concoction, a woman uses loofa gloves to rub your skin - not too harsh, though. Then she leaves and the masseuses come in. It’s an important detail because I expected to tip the masseuses but forgot about the loofa person and was scrambling a bit. After that, there’s a large (free) buffet. I was there for hours. 😂

text.text:
Very polite and heartwarming staff, and truly professional massage.
Before the massage, I filled out a counseling sheet where I could choose the strength and the areas I wanted them to focus on. I also selected aroma oil.

The massage was strong and highly skilled. My whole body felt completely free from stiffness afterward. I was very satisfied with the experience.

text.text: One of the best massage experiences I’ve ever had. From the moment you walk into the spa, there is a lovely energy. All of the staff are incredibly welcoming and friendly, attentive to your preferences and communicate well in English (if you’re concerned about language barriers). My massage therapist was highly skilled and paid attention at all times to my needs. Afterwards we were offered snacks, tea and a seated area to relax once finished. Will 100% return multiple times - Highly recommend, thank you!

text.text: Today I felt so healed and relaxed after the 4-hour healing package at Herbal Spa. The experience was truly top-tier — all the ladies provided wonderful service, and their massage techniques were incredibly skilled. My back and shoulder pain are completely gone, and I feel deeply relaxed. I’m so glad I got to have this experience on the last day of my Da Nang trip.

text.text:
I arrived quite late in the evening, and even though it was already past their closing time, the staff kindly waited for me. That alone left a really good impression.

The overall system reminded me a lot of MiuMiu Spa in Ho Chi Minh City — from the way the service is structured to how attentive the therapists are. therapist maintaining just the right pressure throughout the session. It felt seamless and very professional.

The massage quality was honestly excellent. The use of herbal compresses, hot stones, and essential oils was especially impressive. After the session, they served tea and light snacks, which was a lovely touch. The spa itself was clean and had a calm, relaxing atmosphere — ideal for unwinding after a long day.

Definitely a place I’d return to on my next trip to Da Nang.
